item.title: Learning To Read At Age 35
item.description: At age 35, Oliver James is teaching himself how to read with children’s books, sharing his journey online. Now at a reading level of 8-12 years old, James wants others to know it’s never too late to learn.
item.dc:creator: 60SecondDocs

item.title: Always remember Aitzaz Hassan Bangash Shaheed
item.description: Pakistan will always remember Aitzaz Hassan Bangash Shaheed, the 15 year old who tackled a suicide bomber, sacrificing himself to save so many others. This is to honor Aitzaz. (originally posted by @maureoko on 2023-04-13 08:28:30)

item.title: The guy who peaked in high school.
item.dc:creator: MizWeirdo

item.title: Brazen attempt in San Francisco
item.description: A tow truck driver in downtown San Francisco is attempting to hook up and tow a vehicle in traffic. This is a vehicle being driven by someone sitting in traffic at an intersection waiting on a light to change. The towing company, Speciality Towing, also doesn’t look like it’s much of a reputable business. In February, San Francisco city attorney David Chiu suspended six local towing companies from doing business with the city. Of those six, three — including Specialty Towing — were owned by two individuals, Abigail Fuentes and Jose Badillo. It turns out the pair were up to no good. They were involved in a welfare scheme and lied about their income to receive public assistance according to a statement from Chiu’s office. I'm impressed the Waymo car got out of the way so well.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: This is pure evil: rant/explanation below.
item.description: I just saw this at my local 7/11, and I am enraged. For starters, I'm a pack a day smoker. Have been for about 15 years now. Despite that, I'm well aware tobacco kills. So why does this sign enrage me? A small history lesson. (Note: While I remember events, I don't remember exact dates that well so I may be off in my reckoning by a few years.) So I believe it was back in President Obama's first term that he really cracked down on smoking being peddled to underage minors. They removed flavored cigarettes from the shelves (including my favorite at the time, Djarum Blacks). This was also in the days before vaping was anything other than an incredibly niche hobby. As part of the legislation, a requirement was made that Big Tobacco was required to put a certain percentage of each pack/tin sold to campaigns aimed to help people stop smoking. Great! ...Or at least one would think. The problem is, Big Tobacco was allowed to choose the messaging said campaigns pushed. So instead of the whole cancer/death/birth defects/tooth loss/etc, they went full Mad Men (it's toasted!), and got away with the messaging "quitting is hard". It technically follows the rules (or at least, the rules lobbyists created loopholes for), but what it's actually doing is psychologically reinforcing the belief of those considering quitting that it's just really hard and sowing doubt in those who are on the fence if they want to go through it. So fast forward to last year (or maybe 2 years ago. Everything after 2020 is just 'plague' in my internal reckoning.) As a shock to absolutely nobody, a federal court found Big Tobacco guilty AF of adding a bunch of chemicals to cigarettes and filters to minimize the 'bad' feelings of inhaling burning plant matter, and to increase the addictive properties of tobacco. More than that, it was made a requirement that everywhere selling tobacco products had to have big signs posted at their doors talking about how Big Tobacco has purposefully made their products more addictive, and the issues they cause. Great!! ...or at least one would think. As pictured above, it would appear that Big Tobacco has won again, and are able to have their messaging done AGAIN. So now, instead of smokers reading about all the awful things being done to their bodies, and those near them, we're once again just hit with 'quitting is hard'. This is absolutely insidious, and evil. Big Tobacco once again has won thanks to lobbying. I have no ending to this post beyond shouting in to the void. TL;DR: Big Tobacco has been allowed to control the messaging for legally mandated smoking cessation campaigns, and now are allowed to control the messaging on court-ordered signage that is *supposed* to push people away from smoking.
item.dc:creator: FirefighterCaboose

item.title: Holy hell, you all. I think Joe Biden just paid off my student loans. I have been trying to get the public service loan forgiveness for years. I think I'm going to cry. I was going to be paying those bastards until I died. ?
item.description: Thank you, everyone. I'm still in shock. I can actually think about retiring someday. It's insane. BTW I got Pell grants as well. They hardly covered books and parking. Getting Pell grants means you started behind your more affluent peers and these predatory loan servicers do everything in their power to keep you there. Helping people like me is just a bandaid. The whole system is broken. Dark Brandon wouldn't have had to bail us out if they weren't charging outrageous tuitions and killing us with unconscionable interest rates. Thank you for coming to my Ted Talk.
item.dc:creator: Picdump2508

item.title: Norwegian Forest Cat
item.description: Sorry for Tik Tok. Folks below tell me this is not an NFC, but likely a Maine Coon, but either way, cool AF
item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.description: "Limerence is a state of overwhelming and unexpected longing for emotional reciprocation from another human, known as a limerent object (L.O.), who is often perceived as perfect but unavailable. This may sound similar to the lyrics of a Taylor Swift love song, a scene in “The Great Gatsby,” or the lines in a Shakespeare sonnet. The experience of limerence is timeless, but the term is relatively new. In 1979, Dorothy Tennov, an experimental psychologist and professor at the University of Bridgeport, coined the term limerence in her book “Love and Limerence: The Experience of Being in Love,” based on a decade of research and several hundred case studies on romantic attachment. What differentiates limerence from a crush or love is the intensity, an emotional roller coaster that fluctuates from euphoria to despair. Giulia Poerio, a psychologist and mind-wandering researcher at the University of Sussex in England, said, “Any sign of rejection can make somebody hit a low, and any sign of interest can make somebody hit a high.” It’s an endless mind game of, “She loves me, she loves me not.” Limerents, deeply fearful of rejection, allow their self-esteem to rest in the hands of an L.O. who may not even know they exist. The L.O. is most often a friend, colleague, or stranger met in passing. It can also be someone with whom you’ve had a brief romantic encounter that feels unresolved, explains Dr. Poerio, especially if the L.O. continues to leave breadcrumbs." https://www.nytimes.com/2024/01/27/style/limerence-addiction-love-crush.html Long story short, I'm trying to go 'cold turkey' on my interactions with this woman and it's really rough. I know how I got into this and I'm trying to extricate myself, it's really painful. I sympathize with anyone who might be in this sort of rut, it's truly a mental battle. Probably should have posted this anonymous, but oh well. Edit..... : Um, Great Hera, viral. Wasn't expecting that. Let me just broadly alleviate some concerns I've read in the comments: - In case she is on the internets I am going to be a bit vague. No sense causing her any more grief. This woman recently suffered a trauma, and I was in proximity and reached out to her, so this is limited contact (not friends or former anything, barely acquaintances really). Due to my discovery of her as a person and feelings that developed about her over time I recognized there was a problem and looked into it. - I am speaking to a good friend and she is a therapist, she coined the term and gave me some advice. I put it out here to let others know who might be in the same circumstance and that there are ways to mitigate it if not end it. - I do not intend on any harm or stalking of this person, and haven't seriously done so - any limited obsession I have mitigated as I recognized I was being OCD about her and took steps. - This is much like an addiction, it presents in a way that you seek a type of validation from the LO, to keep that feeling going. You tend to imagine things they say or do that you feel relate to you or not and that drives highs/lows. It works somewhat like any other addiction, but more emotional. - I'm going to be fine, it's sucky right now but I'll work through it. She is moving out of my area so I won't have any interaction with her soon and that will help.
item.dc:creator: Castagere

item.title: True Tree Huger: Julia ;)
item.description: In the late 1990s, Julia Hill climbed a 200-foot, approximately 1000-year-old Californian redwood tree & didn’t come down for another 738 days. She ultimately reached an agreement with Pacific Lumber Company to spare the tree & a 200-foot buffer zone surrounding the tree.

item.title: Dear the Lady who Yelled at my Dog:
item.dc:creator: 42words

item.title: Screaming to tears into the void
item.description: Childcare ratio is 10 to 1 in my age group, and I have to take care of over 20 kids while alone. If the kids clog the toilet, the teachers just leave it to pile up until I notice it. They served bananas with mold on them at lunch that I had to throw away. When I started, they were being investigated for maltreatment. All classes were observed on cameras at lunchtime and I was the only one that washed the tables. On my break, a teacher left the kids alone and was in the bathroom, and two children committed sex acts on another sleeping child. The children have a community blanket for when they forget their own, and the other teachers have never washed it. When I brought these concerns up with another teacher, and told them licensing could shut us down and we could lose our jobs, the director wanted to talk to me. I took the lead in that meeting and told them about all the problems. They then told me that I was out of line for threatening other people with losing their jobs. I got hit hard financially recently and can't just walk out. I reported licensing violations at the center I worked at before. They found out who reported them, and I then found out they were trying to find out how to legally fire me when I was under the protection of whistle blower laws. I can't take another financial hit without being homeless, so I'm waiting until I have another job to report everything. Everyone acts like I'm some kind of drama queen, and my coworkers talk about me when I'm standing within earshot. I've never seen anything like this in my life. I've been in the industry for 2 years, and heard so many horror stories. I break down every day, and hide in the bathroom. I almost threw up from stress the other day, and I can't keep going on with this. I've been applying for other jobs on indeed every day, but I haven't gotten hired anywhere yet. I am the only person trying to fix things, but I have to leave. I didn't want to leave because I feel like the only one that's trying to protect the children. I wish my mom was still around to ask for help. I wish I didn't have to go back in there because I was poor. I talked to someone on the state licensing board and she is helping me get a random visit from a licensor so I can avoid getting named.
item.dc:creator: FoxPeregrine

item.title: Where this Woman at
item.description: Jack Sparrow's a baddie
item.dc:creator: tejayhighlander

item.title: The Void Dumps Back - Episode 13
item.description:
Morning! Let's try to get a piece of this omelet like it's the earlt 2000s, I guess. Friday!
I love this weird saga where strange things are made with jeans.
Obligatory "get in the robot, Shinji".
This is such a cool tree, and the reflectors elevate it. But omg, driving and perhaps being lost at 2am and coming across this would be terrifying.
Nono. HORSIES! Or nothing.
Gonna have to take you shiny, beautiful weirdos in for murder.
I love this so much.
Please?
As the prophecy foretold!
I mean... It IS a crosswalk... On the other hand, far too many people are too comfortable doing this every place they literally should not be doing it.
It me.
Don't be mean to the lovely tortoise!
I haven't been able to get Rock Show - Blink182 out of my head for days, and I'm so annoyed about it.
I love this every time I see it. I'm a simple void.
I'm thinking about it...
Lol I tore so many erasers up by just compulsively doing this.
Good idea, Snorlax. I love you, Snorlax.
Hope everyone's day goes as well as it can, and that there's good stuff in store for you.
item.dc:creator: DaBaDoop

item.title: Wholesome post to brighten your day 1221

I include a Gofundme link in all of my posts to help raise money for people in need. I hope we can help https://www.gofundme.com/f/needing-help-due-to-flesh-eating-disorder
I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support, would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/8M983spH48
I hope this post brightened your day :) and i hope it is a lovely day for you! If you need to talk I am always available on discord (username HaveAGreatDay) and happy to listen. You matter, and if you are struggling please reach out for help. I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support, would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/8M983spH48
